learning_outcomes.description: Clean, tidy, explore, visualize, analyze and develop models to provide insight into large industrial datasets
Explain the opportunities and challenges for advanced modelling approaches in a wide range of system applications, across research, design and operations
Evaluate, select and apply appropriate modelling methodologies for particular outcomes, including both mechanistic and empirical models, and hybrid systems

Critically assess the validity of the assumptions underpinning your model, using data, logic and mechanistic knowledge; assess whether the model is fit for purpose; evaluate the reliability of model output; identify what information is required to improve confidence in model predictions
Apply critical thinking skills to analyze, synthesize and effectively communicate technical information
Demonstrate professional conduct in engaging with industry, including an appreciation of sustainability
Improve teamwork skills through practice, reflection, feedback and change

assessments.task_description:
This course uses real industrial data in project work and assessment.

In order to access this Confidential Information, students are required to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement, in which they agree not to disclose confidential information to any external party, unless required to do so by law. At the end of the course, students are required to permanently erase or physically destroy (by shredding) all Confidential Information received under the Non-Disclosure Agreement. 

The Confidential Information can be described as Process Flow Sheets, Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams, plant operating manuals and process descriptions, plant data and standard operating procedures, regardless of whether such information is designated as “Confidential Information” at the time of its disclosure.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Signed and returned to the course coordinator via the Blackboard submission portal.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Individual exercise 1 Due 12 Mar 17:00 Weight: 10%

Performance on nominated individual case studies and set tasks, from Part A content.

Individual exercise 2 Due 1 May 15:00 Weight: 10%

Performance on nominated individual case studies and set tasks from Part B content.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of the assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission, independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submitted through Turnitin, via the course Blackboard site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Project Presentation and Project Part A
assessments.due_date:
27/03/2026 2:00 pm

2/04/2026 5:00 pm

Single combined submission for CHEE7111 and CHEE7113
assessments.task_description:
Project presentation Part A Due 27/03 14:00 Weight: 5%

Presentation to students, staff and industry personnel. A single, combined presentation will be completed for both the CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 components of the project. Students will be required to mark and respond to presentations by other groups, as well as present their own work. 

Each group will upload a presentation to Blackboard prior to the scheduled presentation time, then either play a recording of their presentation or present live, and then respond live to questions and comments from teaching staff, classmates and industry partner.

Exact timing will depend on the industry partner's availability; the estimated due date is the class time on that date.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in preparing the slides of their oral presentation - see further details about using AI or MT below. However, the oral presentation involving questions and answers is to be delivered in-person and the use of generative AI or MT tools to deliver the oral presentation will not be permitted. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Project report Part A Due 02/04 17:00 Weight: 20%

A team-based project focused on a process system of a complex nature. It will involve both a written submission and a team-based presentation to the client(s).

The single project report will cover Part A projects from both CHEE7111 and CHEE7113.

Submissions will be through Blackboard. A Turnitin link will be made available before the due date.

AI and MT may support students in completing this assessment task - see further details about using AI or MT below. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

Further Details about Using AI or MT

The project presentation and project report involves industry‑supplied documents and/or data. Students must not upload, input, summarise, paraphrase, or otherwise disclose any industry‑supplied information into generative AI or external MT tools under any circumstances. This includes raw data, processed data, figures, tables, text from industry documents, screenshots, file names, or any content that could reveal confidential or proprietary information. Only de‑identified, student‑generated content may be used with AI or MT tools where this is permitted. Breaching this requirement may compromise industry confidentiality and may constitute student misconduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit presentation files via Blackboard. Presentations will be held during class time.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Exam During Exam Period (School) - Individual Oral defence
assessments.task_description:
This is an oral assessment: you will speak on a topic of your choosing for 3 minutes, then defend your comments on that topic and respond to other questions related to the course.

This assessment task evaluates students' abilities, skills and knowledge without the aid of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT). Students are advised that the use of AI or MT technologies to develop responses is strictly prohibited and may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 6(23 Feb - 05 Apr)
learning_activities.topic:
Part A: Data analysis and data driven modelling

All CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 timetabled classes in Week 1 will be devoted to CHEE7111, and in Week 2, all CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 classes will be devoted to CHEE7113. From Week 3 onward, both CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 classes will run each week.

Students will be introduced to key aspects of data analysis and data‑driven modelling during workshop sessions, and will apply these skills through workshops and mentoring activities. Python will be used throughout part A for data processing, analysis, visualisation, and the development of data‑driven models. Content includes systems modelling and data processing, multivariate analysis, time‑series analysis, and the construction, application, and interpretation of data‑driven models. Guest lectures and engagement with the industry partner will occur in parallel with the workshop and mentoring program.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
learning_activities.topic_title: Part A: Data analysis and data driven modelling
learning_activities.topic_description: Part A: Data analysis and data driven modelling All CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 timetabled classes in Week 1 will be devoted to CHEE7111 , and in Week 2, all CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 classes will be devoted to CHEE7113 . From Week 3 onward , both CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 classes will run each week. Students will be introduced to key aspects of data analysis and data‑driven modelling during workshop sessions, and will apply these skills through workshops and mentoring activities. Python will be used throughout part A for data processing, analysis, visualisation, and the development of data‑driven models. Content includes systems modelling and data processing, multivariate analysis, time‑series analysis, and the construction, application, and interpretation of data‑driven models. Guest lectures and engagement with the industry partner will occur in parallel with the workshop and mentoring program.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 13(13 Apr - 31 May)
learning_activities.topic:
Part B: Modelling application to control

All CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 timetabled classes in Week 7 will be devoted to CHEE7111, and in Week 8 all CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 classes will be devoted to CHEE7113. From Week 9 onward, both CHEE7111 and CHEE7113 classes will run each week.

Students will be introduced to the fundamental principles of good modelling practice in both empirical and mechanistic modelling during workshop sessions, and will apply these skills through workshops and mentoring activities. Python and MATLAB/Simulink will be used throughout part B for model calibration, assessment, visualisation, and implementation in process control applications. Content includes best‑practice approaches to model development, model evaluation, communicating results, and applying models to advanced control strategies. Guest lectures and engagement with the industry partner will occur in parallel with the workshop and mentoring program, supporting students as they complete individual assessment tasks and an applied industry project delivered in coordination with CHEE7113.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05, L06, L07
